title: Board Library for esp8266 2.5.0-beta2 does not work with WiFiClientSecure
body:
Hello Joel, just to let you know:
I recently upgraded the esp8266 board library to 2.5.0-beta2
Your library works for normal connections, but not for TLS using WiFiClientSecure, if the above mentioned board library is installed.

In the log of my mosquitto broker (on Raspberry Pi) I see, that the device is trying to connect:
`New connection from 192.168.11.84 on port 8883`
On the device  side, client.connect() never becomes true.
I tried to ignore this test and submitted a payload to a topic on the broker, it never arrived there.
It seems safe to assume, that the connection somehow is not correctly working

I downgraded to 2.4.2, everything is back to normal and working flawlessly.

From what I read on the github page of the ESP8266 project, they did a lot of work under the hood. Probably it is best to wait a few days or even weeks before digging into this, but this is up to you.

title: Causes crashes in OctoPrint's login when disabled
body:
I just had a user report an odd issue in foosel/OctoPrint#2442. They have the Astroprint plugin disabled, and that makes them not able to login anymore.

A closer look at OctoPrint's plugin subsystem reveals that contrary to what I so far assumed, plugins are still loaded even when disabled. Which means that in your case the plugin's implementation will be initialized, and in its constructor functions will be attached to Flask Login's loggedIn and loggedOut signals that try to send messages to the frontend via `self._plugin_manager` - however, if your plugin is disabled, your plugin won't be fully initialized, `self._plugin_manager` hence not injected and that means things crash inside Flask Login when trying to call your signal handler and the login fails with an HTTP 500.

I'll have to do something about this on OctoPrint's side, however I strongly suggest to not attach any stuff to the system as long as the plugin is not fully initialized (read: the `initialized` method on it is called). 

Could you fix this on your end? As I said, I'll also look into finding a good fix on mine. If push comes to shove, disabled plugins will simply get the same treatment that blacklisted ones get, meaning some metadata will be missing in the plugin list but no code will be executed at all, preventing such issues in the future.

I've updated to latest Azure IoT SDK on my device from 1.1.14 to 1.1.21 and experienced the following issue: it looks like IoT Hub SDK for C is unable to reconnect after connection loss over MQTT protocol, for example, when it loses Internet access.

title: Mqtt not timing out messages when can't reconnect
body:
Initially opened on https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-python/issues/216

If MQTT is unable to establish a connection, it will keep trying and once it succeeds queued messages will be sent to the Cloud.

However, if for some reason we can't get past the initial connection phase, then SDK does not respect message timeouts.  An easy way to repro this is to just send a few messages with networking enabled via convenience sample, then disable your network in middle.

What is happening is that in `IoTHubTransport_MQTT_Common_DoWork`, we have logic that will compare max # of retries and ultimately call `sendMsgComplete(mqttMsgEntry->iotHubMessageEntry, transport_data, IOTHUB_CLIENT_CONFIRMATION_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT);` which tells caller that we should timeout.

However - if the initial call to `InitializeConnection` at the top of this function fails, then we never get into the correct spot in the loop to check the timeouts.  

Note there is at IoTHub_Client layer a `DoTimeouts` API.  However it doesn't save us here because its already transferred the messages from its pending queue to MQTT's waitingForAck.

To fix this
A) Checking for message timeouts should not be conditional on state of MQTT itself.  And 
B) It feels more natural to be the timeout based on actual timing, instead of checking whether a retry has occurred or not.  
C) The code in the block `Codes_SRS_IOTHUB_TRANSPORT_MQTT_COMMON_07_057` also needs review.  After we timeout the 1st timed out message, it looks like we unconditionally run through the queued messages and reset their retryCount=0.  If we just did (B) though, this would be moot.

title: Add a way to check on low-level AMQP or MQTT connect failures
body:
- **OS and version used:** Linux

- **SDK version used:** release_2018_10_03

# Description of the issue:
The problem to be solved is that the attempt to connect to Azure sometimes fails at the low level, and the low level debug messages make this plain, but nothing is communicated back to the high level functions that we are using. (Either they return void, or the cases where they return an opaque handle only fail when something seriously bad happens, like a malloc failure or null input argument.) Examples would be an unauthorized ConnectionString or bad proxy settings. Without this, much time passes until you find that you can’t send messages; that is your clue that the connection was not opened.

The callback for the Connection Status change is not being invoked here, presumably because it has not yet *changed*.

Looking at the existing Azure APIs, none is quite right for getting at this. Adding a getter for the Connection Status would be one option, though that may be a fair amount of work since the mocking function must also be provided, and tests to validate it.

What I chose to do was to patch the closest of the existing APIs, GetSendStatus. Normally this returns IOTHUB_CLIENT_OK if it is able to get the SendStatus (which is either Idle or Busy, returned as an output parameter). My simple patch was to return IOTHUB_CLIENT_ERROR from the low level IoTHubTransport_AMQP_Common_GetSendStatus() function if transport’s state is not Connected or Connecting.

I think a better choice, if still using the same API signature for GetSendStatus, would be to extend the set of enums from just Idle and Busy to add NotConnected or somesuch. The justification for leaning on this API could be that NotConnected is an indication of "why you can’t Send right now".

# Code sample exhibiting the issue:
My patch, for AMQP over websockets, is provided here. I recommend adding the new enum instead of what I did, but the patch shows the way. I think MQTT will have a similar solution.
[ll_ampq_extensions.patch.txt](https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-c/files/2634678/ll_ampq_extensions.patch.txt)

title: Segfault, probably use-after-free, when deleting client shortly after creation
body:
<!--
Hi there! thank you for discovering and submitting an issue!

Please use your Azure subscription if you need to share any information from your Azure subscription such as connection strings, service names (IoTHub, Provisioning), etc.

# Need Support?
* Have a feature request for SDKs? Please post it on [User Voice](https://feedback.azure.com/forums/321918-azure-iot) to help us prioritize.
* Have a technical question? Ask on [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure-iot-hub) with tag "azure-iot-hub".
* Need Support? Every customer with an active Azure subscription has access to [support](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-supportability/how-to-create-azure-support-request) with guaranteed response time.  Consider submitting a ticket and get assistance from Microsoft support team
* Found a bug? Please help us fix it by thoroughly documenting it and filing an issue on GitHub ([C](https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-c), [Java](https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-java), [.NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-csharp), [Node.js](https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-node), [Python](https://github.com/Azure/azure-iot-sdk-python)).

Please first tell us a little bit about the environment you're running:
-->


- **OS and version used:** Windows 10 <!-- Windows 10, Ubuntu 15.04... -->

- **SDK version used:** 2018-12-13 <!-- Please include the SDK version -->


# Description of the issue:
<!-- Please be as detailed as possible: which feature has a problem, how often does it fail,  -->
When creating a number of clients using AMQP and then destroying the clients shortly afterwards, a seg-fault is triggered. Most likely it is triggered by a use-after-free and possibly race-conditions.

# Code sample exhibiting the issue:
<!-- Please remove any connection string information! -->
```c++
#include "azure_c_shared_utility/platform.h"
#include "iothub_client.h"
#include "iothubtransportamqp.h"
#include <chrono>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <ctime>
#include <iostream>
#include <map>
#include <string>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

static std::map<std::string, std::string> id_key_map{
    // Map containing 16 device IDs and keys};

static const char *hub_name = "[hub-name]";
static const char *hub_suffix = "azure-devices.net";

static void connection_status(IOTHUB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_STATUS result,
                              IOTHUB_CLIENT_CONNECTION_STATUS_REASON reason,
                              void *context)
{
    std::cout << "Connection status reason = " << reason << std::endl;
}

static int on_device_method(const char *method_name,
                            const unsigned char *payload, size_t size,
                            METHOD_HANDLE method_id, void *context)
{
    std::cout << "Device method " << method_name << " called" << std::endl;
    return 0;
}

static IOTHUBMESSAGE_DISPOSITION_RESULT
iothub_client_message(IOTHUB_MESSAGE_HANDLE msg, void *context)
{
    std::cout << "Message received!" << std::endl;
    return IOTHUBMESSAGE_ACCEPTED;
}

IOTHUB_CLIENT_HANDLE create(TRANSPORT_HANDLE transport, std::string device,
                            std::string key)
{
    IOTHUB_CLIENT_CONFIG config;
    memset(&config, 0, sizeof(config));
    config.deviceId = device.c_str();
    config.deviceKey = key.c_str();
    config.protocol = AMQP_Protocol;
    auto retval = IoTHubClient_CreateWithTransport(transport, &config);
    IoTHubClient_SetConnectionStatusCallback(retval, connection_status,
                                             nullptr);
    IoTHubClient_SetMessageCallback(retval, iothub_client_message, nullptr);
    IoTHubClient_SetDeviceMethodCallback_Ex(retval, on_device_method, nullptr);

    return retval;
}

void destroy(IOTHUB_CLIENT_HANDLE handle)
{
    IoTHubClient_SetConnectionStatusCallback(handle, nullptr, nullptr);
    IoTHubClient_SetMessageCallback(handle, nullptr, nullptr);
    IoTHubClient_SetDeviceMethodCallback_Ex(handle, nullptr, nullptr);
    IoTHubClient_Destroy(handle);
}

int main(int argc, char **argv)
{
    using namespace std::chrono_literals;
    std::srand(std::time(nullptr));
    platform_init();

    auto transport =
        IoTHubTransport_Create(AMQP_Protocol, hub_name, hub_suffix);

    while (true)
    {
        std::vector<IOTHUB_CLIENT_HANDLE> clients;
        std::cout << "Creating clients..." << std::endl;
        for (const auto &p : id_key_map)
        {
            auto c = create(transport, p.first, p.second);
            clients.push_back(c);
        }

        // I can reliably crash the program on my machine using
        // a 100ms sleep. Longer sleeps takes longer to crash
        // but eventually the program will crash.
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(100ms);

        std::cout << "Deleting clients..." << std::endl;
        for (auto p : clients)
        {
            destroy(p);
        }
        auto sleep_time = std::chrono::seconds{5};
        std::cout << "Sleeping for " << sleep_time.count() << " seconds"
                  << std::endl;
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(sleep_time);
    }
}
```

The crash happens in function `update_state` in `iothubtransport_amqp_cbs_auth.c`

# Console log of the issue:
<!-- Please share as much logs as posible, that will help debugging -->
<!-- Don't forget to remove any connection string information! -->

title: Desired twin updates gets reverted when a connection is restored
body:
- Windows 10.0.17763.194
- Azure C-SDK 1.2.10

# Description of the issue:
I’m seeing a very weird issue – basically, I do the following:
 
- Client gets connected and receives the full device twin as expected.
- Iunplug the network cable of the device. 
   - The client SDK output shows that it lost connectivity and is retrying. The pause between each try increases as expected.
- I update the device twin desired properties using Device Explorer. 
    - Hitting refresh shows the new values as expected.
- I plug-in the network cable again. 
    - The client SDK output shows that it regained connectivity.
    - I wait a few seconds, and it successfully gets the entire device twin again.
 
Now, at this point, I expect to get the updates I made in #3 – however, I get the old version. Moreover, if I look at the desired properties using Device Explorer, I see my changes from #3 got reverted. The version stamp remains the same – but the values are the old one.

- **OS and version used:** Ubuntu 18.04 <!-- Windows 10, Ubuntu 15.04... -->

- **SDK version used:** Release Dec. 13, 2018 <!-- Please include the SDK version -->
- **Target:** ESP32.

# Description of the issue:
<!-- Please be as detailed as possible: which feature has a problem, how often does it fail,  -->
I am trying to connect the ESP32 to my Blob storage. I am getting an HTTP error 401 (unauthorized access).
I am using the example: iothub_client_sample_upload_to_blob_mb.
I tried connecting using just the Shared Access Key in my connection string, but this did not work (no connection). After that I generated an SAS token in Azure (Storage Accounts -> <account name> -> Shared Access Signature) and plugged that in into my connection string.
My connection string looks like this: 
`static const char* connectionString = "HostName=<Host name>;DeviceId=<Device ID>;SharedAccessSignature=<inserted here without the "?" at the beginning>";
`
Q1: Why is there a "?" in front of the token? When I look at the connection string, at SharedAccessSignature=.. I don't see the "?".

I also set up the Endpoint in Azure under IoT Hub -> Upload files.
In the example, I am using the option `SET_TRUSTED_CERT_IN_SAMPLES`.

Q2: What does that mean? I am not so familiar with basic encryption and should probably read up on that.

Q3: Why am I getting an 401 error? What  could be a possible solution?

title: segmentation fault in Java binding's Module_Start
body:
Hi team,
thank you for great work and quick response!

I saw segmentation fault (or access violation in Windows) from `Gateway_CreateFromJson`. The debugger said that the exception had been thrown from jvm.dll which had been called https://github.com/Azure/iot-edge/blob/master/bindings/java/src/java_module_host.c#

### Environment

* Windows 10 x64 with CU
* Using NuGet package (https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Azure.Devices.Gateway.Native.Windows.x64/)
   * I manually extracted the dll and pdb from the package's `runtime` folder to investigate.
* Oracle JDK 8u 131

### Additional information:

* My code calls `Broker#publish()` and wait for "response message" for request-response style messaging for initialization in `GatewayModule#start()`.
* When I commented out `Broker#publish()` call from `GatewayModule#start()`, the segfault looked disappear.
* As of our logging code in Java, `GatewayModule#start()` finished successfully.
* Another module sent messages to the Java module continuously.
* The debugger said that the invalid address is almost '0x270' and '0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF` once.
    * I failed to check call stack when I saw '0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF`. 

### Hypothesis

As far as I look, I found similar error in [Xamarin Android](https://bugzilla.xamarin.com/show_bug.cgi?id=13521#c17), it is race condition of `JNIEnv*`.
So, my hypothesis of this issue is race condition of `env` field of `MODULE_JAVA_DATA` as following:

1. The java host module initializes `JAVA_MODULE_HANDLE_DATA` as module data in [this line](https://github.com/Azure/iot-edge/blob/master/bindings/java/src/java_module_host.c#L116).
2. The `module_worker` created from the broker, passing `module_info` in [this line](https://github.com/Azure/iot-edge/blob/master/core/src/broker.c#L373).
3. The `JavaModuleHost_Start` calls JNI's `AttachCurrentThread` and stores `JNIEnv*` in the field of `module` argument in [this line](https://github.com/Azure/iot-edge/blob/master/bindings/java/src/java_module_host.c#L473). I will call this thread as "main-thread".
4. The `module_worker` calles `JavaModuleHost_Receive` simultaneously, and it also calls JNI's `AttachCurrentThread` and then stores `JNIEnv*` in the field of `module` argument in [this line](https://github.com/Azure/iot-edge/blob/master/bindings/java/src/java_module_host.c#L407). This step overwrites `JNIEnv*` for "main-thread". I will call this thread as "worker-thread"
5. The `GatewayModule#start()` is finished, then ``JavaModuleHost_Start` calls JNI's `ExceptionOccurred` with overwritten. It causes segmentation fault.
    * If the "worker-thread" already called `DetachCurrentThread`, it is reasonable the `env` field was invalidated with jvm as `-1L`.

So I think that `JNIEnv*` should be stored in the local variable instead of "effectively global" variable in the struct, ant it may solve this problem. In fact, it just works in our testing environment.

Any idea?

Thank you for always.

title: temperature and humidity sensor does not read [solved]
body:
I am testing the temp and humidity sensor that comes with the starter kit (the blue one).

The example code in the projects folder gives back nans. Here is the minimum code that produces the error, where the temp is printed once but not again:

```
from grovepi import *
from grove_oled import *

dht_sensor_port = 7		# Connect the DHt sensor to port 7
time.sleep(.1)

while True:
	try:
		[ temp,hum ] = dht(dht_sensor_port,1)		#Get the temperature and Humidity from the DHT sensor

		print("temp =", temp, "C\thumidity =", hum,"%") 
		time.sleep(1)	
	except (IOError,TypeError) as e:
		print("Error")

```
This is the output
```
pi@raspberrypi:~ $ python3 home_temp_hum_display.py 
temp = 28.0 C	humidity = 47.0 %
temp = nan C	humidity = nan %
temp = nan C	humidity = nan %

```

 However, I discovered that this does work:

```
from grovepi import *
from grove_oled import *

dht_sensor_port = 7		# Connect the DHt sensor to port 7

time.sleep(.1)

while True:
	try:
		dht(dht_sensor_port,0)
		time.sleep(0.5)
		[ temp,hum ] = dht(dht_sensor_port,1)		#Get the temperature and Humidity from the DHT sensor

		print("temp =", temp, "C\thumidity =", hum,"%") 
		time.sleep(1)	
	except (IOError,TypeError) as e:
		print("Error")
```

title: Bug in routing
body:
From express/server.js - you set the routes like
'/db' - routeDatabase
'/device' - routeDevice
'/' - routeDeviceRegistration

'/db/search' for device search
'/db/{0}' where device definition

'/device/xxx' - device discovery/set/get values

'/xxx' - adapter subscription

The problem really occurs with the last one - where 'xxx' is defined as '/:adapterName/yyy'

What if I was very bad and decided to name my adapter "db" or "device"?!?  Routing would get really screwed up since I was able to match a different route.  Same goes if you change the device ID to a string - then I could have a device id of "search" and screw that part up.

The routes should be more explicit - something like
'/db/search'
'/db/get'
'/device/discovery/xxx'
'/device/get/xxx'
'/device/set/xxx'
'/device/subscribe/xxx'
'/device/unsubscribe/xxx'

That way they 'dynamic' part of the route can't screw up your routing.

title: [bug] we do not handle a vlaue of type 'short' correctly.
body:
**Version**

Build/Run method
- [ ] Docker
- [ ] PKG
- [x] Manually built (git clone - npm install - npm run build ) 

Zwave2Mqtt version: master @ 20190805
Openzwave Version: 1.6.848

**Describe the bug**
Once of the devices I have is missing configuration in OpenZwave, while in the progess of writing a config file for it. I noticed values of type`short` are not handled properly.

**To Reproduce**
Per the manual, parameter 10 should be `short`:
https://manuals.fibaro.com/content/manuals/en/FGWOEF-011/FGWOEF-011-T-EN-v1.0.pdf

![param10](https://imgur.com/PXCOp6e.png)

```xml
    <Value type="short" index="10" genre="config" label="LED frame - power limit" value="36800" min="500" max="36800" size="4">
      <Help>This parameter determines maximum active power. Exceeding it results in the LED frame flashing violet. Function is active only when parameter 11 is set to 8 or 9.
500-36800 (50.0-3680.0W, step 0.1W) - power threshold</Help>
    </Value>
```

With `short` (above) it shows up as a negative value, when I switch to `int` (below) it shows up correctly with the default value of '36800'

```xml
    <Value type="int" index="10" genre="config" label="LED frame - power limit" value="36800" min="500" max="36800" size="4">
      <Help>This parameter determines maximum active power. Exceeding it results in the LED frame flashing violet. Function is active only when parameter 11 is set to 8 or 9.
500-36800 (50.0-3680.0W, step 0.1W) - power threshold</Help>
    </Value>
```

I think it might be a signed vs unsigned issues here, not sure if is in zwave2mqtt or in node-shared-openzwave. But accoriding to https://github.com/OpenZWave/open-zwave/wiki/Adding-Devices a `short` is the correct type to use as it more closely matches the range. 

```
o int - A integer can represent a number from 0 to 4294967295
o short - A Short can represent a number from 0 to 65535
```

**Expected behavior**
Values of type short to be disaplyed correctly.

**Additional context**
n/a

Zwave2Mqtt version: 2.0.2

**Describe the bug**
It is not possible to disable node polling once that it has been enabled.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Enable node polling for a Z-Wave node: Configuration -> Gateway -> New Value -> Choose any value V from one of the nodes N and check the "Poll" checkbox -> Save
2. Check the debug logs to verify that the node N value V is being actively polled by zwave2mqtt
3. Disable node value polling: Configuration -> Gateway -> Disable polling of the value from step 1 by editing the entry and unchecking "Poll" -> Save
4. zwave2mqtt continues to poll the node N for the value V from step 1

**Expected behavior**
zwave2mqtt no longer polls node N for value V

title: WiFi Provisioning: drop WiFI AP after getting config command
body:
We're facing provisioning issues of SparkFun Blynk Board from iOS devices. From the logs it is seen as the board just can't connect:
```
SSID: freud
Pass: annafreud2013
Auth: 04b3b38ab7bb4ffab779d3275e620d6c
Host: qa.blynk.cc
Port: 8442
Connecting to qa.blynk.cc
Connecting to: freud
..............
Timed out connecting to WiFi.
```
I've noticed, that in such case, the iPhone is still connected to board's WiFI AP (e.g. "BlynkMe-BBYR"). This seems to be an issue.

This is not the case with Android, because Android drops connection to board after sending `config` http request. Unfortunately, there is no way to do the same on iOS.

As experiment, we've tried to provision board from Android without explicitly dropping connection to it after `config` http request. The result was the same as on iOS: board couldn't connect to instructed WiFi network.

title: Overwriting socket data / not waiting for a response
body:
Discovered this issue when working on a separate project that depends on TuyAPI.

(I believe this may be the root cause of https://github.com/codetheweb/tuyapi/issues/158 and https://github.com/codetheweb/tuyapi/issues/103.)

I added a condition to the data event handler for the socket that checks if the parsed and returned data is an object; if not it would throw an error.  After adding this, tests started failing.

I discovered that on a fast enough machine (and testing against a local stub) it's possible to overwrite data sent to the socket by calling `.set()` and/or `.get()` quickly after each other.  Data has already been being overwritten in the test suite, but it wasn't caught because I didn't add a check to see if the returned data was an object or not.

For instance, running the test suite generated this beautiful packet:

```

```

~~Obviously invalid~~.  I took a stab at implementing a [function queue system](https://github.com/codetheweb/tuyapi/tree/patch-response-bad-data), but it's not working.

Thoughts @tjfontaine @kueblc @unparagoned?

title: Dnsmasq conflict with systemd-resolved on Lubuntu 18.04
body:
Hi,

When launching start_flash.sh on Lubuntu 18.04, I have an error in the log file :
Dnsmasq cannot strat because address is already in use.

And effectively, the port 53 is already used by systemd-resolved.

To solve this problem, I had to edit scripts/setup.asp and add the following line to /etc/dnsmasq.conf, juste after interface=$WLAN 👍
bind-interfaces
listen-address=10.42.42.1

This allowes Dnsmasq to start on a different IP than systemd-resolved.

Fred

title: Handling Override off->on when triggered by EC
body:
## Description
It's possible that I'm misunderstanding the expected behavior, but I'm not sure the overrides are behaving properly. 

## Configuration
```yaml
entity_controller:
  closet:
    sensor: binary_sensor.0x00158d0003cc4255_occupancy
    entity: light.shelly1_2c76fa_relay_0
    sensor_type: duration
    delay: 30
    overrides:
      - sensor.foo
```
## Steps to reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Trigger motion sensor (light turns on)
2. Set override state (`sensor.foo`) to on

### Expected behavior
EC stops changing the state; the light that was on remains on forever.
(if the motion sensor was not triggered before `sensor.off` went from `off` to `on`, the light should stay off forever)

### Actual Behaviour
EC immediately shuts off the light
(if the motion sensor was not triggered before `sensor.off` went from `off` to `on`, the light does in fact stay off forever)

title: MQTT update device failed while api OK
body:
Hi 

Currently in version 4.10213, I am having a problem with mqtt update cmd.

I am controlling plugs from mqtt without any issue (using switchligth cmd).

I tried controlling my westinghouse fan using the same principle but it fails.

While the API version is working:

`http://xxxx/json.htm?type=command&param=switchlight&idx=41&switchcmd=med`

The equivalent with MQTT `{"command":"switchlight","idx":41,"switchcmd":"med"}` is not valid which is coherent with controls done in https://github.com/domoticz/domoticz/blob/3741a530c2260cea2f337a095aa9477847fc00cb/hardware/MQTT.cpp#L253

So I change in flavor of `udevice` cmd.

I convert the API working JSON into the MQTT cmd.
it gives something like
```json
{"command":"udevice","idx":41,"nvalue":2}
```

Both are creating following JSON in domoticz/out:
```json
{"Battery":255,"RSSI":12,"description":"","dtype":"Fan","id":"000000","idx":41,"name":"Fan","nvalue":2,"stype":"Westinghouse","switchType":"On/Off","unit":0}
```

However, while the API is working fine the MQTT version does not work.

Am I doing something wrong? Is there a known issue?

title: closed AMQP 1.0 consumer after idle timeout is not restored
body:
Azure Service Bus has the behavior that idle AMQP 1.0 links are closed after ~10 minutes of idle timeout.

We get following log message and afterwards the AMQP 1.0 Azure Service Bus connection is in a strange state:
```
Consumer <org.apache.qpid.jms.JmsMessageConsumer@3b8f105d> closed due to JmsConnectionRemotelyClosedException: The connection was inactive for more than the allowed 120000 milliseconds and is closed by container '...'. [condition = amqp:connection:forced]
```

"Strange" because: after that message we get following messages:
```
May 23rd 2019, 06:56:14.973 Session closed: org.apache.qpid.jms.JmsSession@3230bc47 - Unknown error from remote peer
May 23rd 2019, 06:56:14.975 Connection interrupted: amqps://<...>.servicebus.windows.net:5671?amqp.saslMechanisms=PLAIN&transport.trustAll=true&transport.verifyHost=false
May 23rd 2019, 06:56:16.229 Connection restored: amqps://<...>.servicebus.windows.net:5671?amqp.saslMechanisms=PLAIN&transport.trustAll=true&transport.verifyHost=false
```

The last 2 messages repeat themselves each second.

We could/should react on `org.eclipse.ditto.services.connectivity.messaging.amqp.AmqpClientActor.StatusReportingListener#onConsumerClosed` and restore the connection.

What also can be seen: the default JMS client timeout seems to be 120s (see error message above) - we should increase that to at least 5 min.
Azure Service Bus' timeout seems to be ~9-11 minutes.

I'm not sure why this only happens on Service Bus - as for Eclipse Hono we also have consumers which are "idling" 2 minutes. Maybe the AMQP endpoint does however keep them open infinitely.

@kaizimmerm : could you please have a look on this?

4. Add User:read permission to user "123"

title: Build failing on Windows machine
body:
**Describe the bug**
The build of latest `develop` fails when running `mvn clean install -DskipTests -Pconsole,docker` command on Windows. 
**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Checkout a new branch, up-to-date with the latest `develop`
2. Run `mvn clean install -DskipTests -Pconsole,docker` 
3. The build fails with **The system cannot find the file specified** error in directory `kapua\assembly\api`

**Expected behavior**
The build should pass without errors.

**Screenshots**
_Screenshot 1:_
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35954696/72446312-a0858500-37b3-11ea-9e7e-bfe4c8afcd49.png)

_Screenshot 2_
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35954696/72446367-ba26cc80-37b3-11ea-8ac6-2d39efcba374.png)

**Version of Kapua**
1.1.0

**Type of deployment**
[ ] Local Vagrant deployment
[X] Docker
[ ] Openshift (in its variants) 
[ ] Others

**Main component affected**
[ ] Console (in case of console please report info on which browser you encountered the problem)
[ ] REST API
[ ] Message Broker
[X] - Others

**Additional context**
Discussion about this error was started on gitter, and as agreed with @lorthirk a new issue is opened.

title: Throw KapuaNotFoundException if device does not exist
body:
When calling Device Events REST API, the response returns an empty list when the Device does not exist. With this PR a `KapuaEntityNotFoundException` will be returned instead, notifying that the Device could not be found.

**Related Issue**
No related issues

title: Fix metric valueType on JsonKapuaPayload

Metrics in `JsonKapuaPayload` always have a `string` value type, no matter the real type of the metric. This PR fixes this.

Fixes #2744

name: OPC-UA DRIVER - Return a String Array

title: Module outputs are not available in scripts
body:
The values of outputs of other modules are made available to script, which is also directly supported by the rule editor:

![screen shot 2017-01-18 at 10 15 23](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3244965/22057952/507b7474-dd67-11e6-88b9-7b4b69045d65.png)

When trying to access those outputs, an exception is thrown.

The problem is that in the rule context, they are made available as e.g. "1.newState", which is provided as a map 1->newState in within the rule execution context. It is probably rather a bad idea to define "1" as a variable name...

Advanced dynamically added channels are not hidden in Paper UI
body:
For #2663 I am dynamically adding advanced channels (`colorzone`, `temperaturezone`) to `colormzlight` Things. That works well, however Paper UI does not hide these `advanced` channels when configuring or controlling a Thing.

I did some research and to me it seems Paper UI uses the response from the `thing-types` REST service to determine if channels should be hidden. 

However the dynamic channel types (`colorzone`, `temperaturezone`) are missing from the response of http://localhost:8080/rest/thing-types/lifx:colormzlight :

title: [REST/Persistence] API request for persistence items returns empty list
body:
I installed two persistence extensions: rrd4j and InfluxDB.

A GET request to "/rest/persistence" returns the following result:

`[
  {
    "id": "rrd4j",
    "label": "rrd4j",
    "type": "Queryable"
  },
  {
    "id": "influxdb",
    "label": "influxdb",
    "type": "Queryable"
  }
]`

A GET request to "/rest/persistence/items?serviceId=rrd4j" and "/rest/persistence/items?serviceId=influxdb" returns an empty list with status code 200:

`[]`

Expected result: A list of all available items as defined in the *.rules file.

The data for a specific item is available at "/rest/persistence/items/{ITEMID}?serviceId=influxdb".

The behaviour can be reproduced with a clean install of openHAB 2.0.0.

title: NPE in ThingManager at startup
body:
I upgraded to OH build 1271 (from 1250), which I believe includes the most recent ESH stable build 270.

I consistently see multiple NPEs (4 to be precise) in ThingManager during startup.

@htreu Could the recent changes to `BundleResolver` result in `getBundleName` being called before `bundleResolver` has been set?
https://github.com/eclipse/smarthome/commit/5847376494a201803aa526c675522cfa9ddde3de#diff-24d71ac793f6de6c8557ea06292497ddR978

```

title: postUpdate/sendCommand not synchronous in Rules
body:
migrated from Bugzilla [#478435](https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=478435)
status UNCONFIRMED severity _normal_ in component _DSL_ for _---_
Reported in version _0.8.0_ on platform _PC_
Assigned to: Project Inbox

On 2015-09-25 12:47:15 -0400, karel goderis wrote:

> With the following simple rule, and with the Item TeslaScheduledDeparture holding a valid value (e.g. not NULL or UNDEF):
> 
> rule "Tesla - initiate variables rule"
> when 
>   System started
> then
>   TeslaScheduledDeparture.postUpdate(UnDefType.UNDEF)
>   logInfo("org.openhab","System Started : TeslaScheduledDeparture "+TeslaScheduledDeparture.state)
> end
> 
> the following is written to the logs:
> 
> 2015-09-25 18:40:12 [DEBUG] [.m.r.r.i.engine.RuleEngineImpl:267  ] - Executing startup rule 'Tesla - initiate variables rule'
> 2015-09-25 18:40:14 [INFO ] [o.e.s.model.script.org.openhab:48   ] - System Started : TeslaScheduledDeparture 2015-09-26T08:30:00
> 
> Does this async behaviour stem from the introduction of the safeCall()s? 
> 
> This is imho undesired behaviour, and leads to adverse effects in Rules. The workaround is to copy over Item.state(s) to locally defined variables at the beginning of each, to do the necessary processing, and at the exit of a rule, write back/postUpdate() any values to the Item.state(s)
> 
> Therefore, should lock()s be introduced on the ItemRegistry?

On 2015-09-26 03:40:32 -0400, Dennis Nobel wrote:

> > Does this async behaviour stem from the introduction of the safeCall()s? 
> 
> No, the safeCalls are executed synchronously. Only if a call takes longer than 10 seconds, the thread will continue to work.
> 
> ItÂ´s because a postUpdate is by definition asynchronously. The whole eventing system only allows asynchronous events.

On 2015-09-26 04:34:11 -0400, karel goderis wrote:

> mhh... do you think this would not lead to problems then? like, you are never sure to have the latest value of an Item when you call .state 
> 
> Is there a recommended approach then for Rules?
> 
> Karel

On 2015-09-26 05:52:39 -0400, Dennis Nobel wrote:

> It depends. Do you really want to query the state of an item directly after you have updated it in a rule? If you sent a command you either donÂ´t know how long it will take before the binding changes the state. The behavior - independent from the eventing system - is just asynchronously.
> 
> What is your concrete use case?
> 
> If you want to get informed that a value was changed you can use the newly introduced ItemStateChangedEvent. This event is sent AFTER an item state was updated.

On 2015-09-26 05:59:06 -0400, karel goderis wrote:

> I understand, but in this case the binding is not the limiting factor. it also happens with Items that are not bound to a binding.
> 
> ItemStateChangedEvent is not yet exposed to the ScriptEngine right?
> 
> K

On 2015-09-26 06:08:40 -0400, Dennis Nobel wrote:

> No itÂ´s not. But I think we donÂ´t need it there, as the Rule engine already has something like "Item MyItem changed" as a trigger. Even if the state of an Item might not be changed at this time, I think usually there is no problem, because you just work with the value from the update event inside the rule.

On 2015-09-26 07:19:08 -0400, karel goderis wrote:

> Ah - that I am afraid does not hold true. At least in my case - you do not only need the value that has effectively changed, but also the latest value of all the there Items, either in or out "Item changed" clauses.
> 
> You either have to build very long complex Rules, with a trigger for every possible variable/Item you need in the rule, or you make a bunch of rules that are less complex, acting on a single Item. In such a constellation, if you update the value of an Item in Rule A, you do need that exact value maybe in Rule B. 
> 
> I can tell you from the use case I have here (automatic charging and pre-heating rules for my Tesla), that these "glitches" are very annoying and troublesome. And that is only relatively simple use case imho
> 
> Karel

title: Synchronous Execution of Astro Startup Jobs and minor fixes
body:
The scheduling operations schedules few jobs and executes 2 specific jobs immediately. These two jobs are executed in a different thread to prevent blocking the thread that spawns it. 

In addition, during handler initialize, channel linking and unlinking, we reschedule the jobs. And every time, before we reschedule the jobs, we remove the jobs from the ESH Core scheduler that are associated with the current thing.

As the jobs that execute immediately are handled by different threads, it is sometime noticed that while rescheduling, removal of jobs did occur after the jobs got rescheduled. This created unexpected issues in ESH Core scheduler. In this PR, we have rendered the asynchronous operations of executing immediate jobs in different threads to synchronous execution so that we can make sure the removal always happens before rescheduling.

Another note is that previously we required `equals` and `hashcode` to be overriden by `AbstractJob` but currently it is not used anymore. These `equals` and `hashcode` affected normal executions of scheduled jobs as well. Hence, the methods have been removed.

body:

I see that in 'button_context', gpio_pin is declared as '**_uint8_t_**', which results in a problem when an offset of 512 is added along with the digital pin number to initialize gpio in button_init().

Shouldn't this be declared as **_'int_**'?

title: Bug in interrupt handling without storing DSP/FPU registers
body:
# Issue Summary
- **Type**: *Bug* 
- **Category**: *BSP* 
- **Release Version**: Specified version such as 2016.05 or All

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Bug

## Development Environment
- **HOST OS**: Windows 10, 64bit

- **TOOLCHAIN**: MWDT L-2017.06

- **BOARD**: EMSK 2.3

- **ARC CORE**: Any

## Bug Description
   FPU and DSP registers are not saved during handling of interrupts and exceptions, This may lead to undefined behavior.
For my case: I have a thread that has a division operation, and there is a **vKernelTick** which is the ISR of tick timer of freertos port. This interrupt has also a division operation in calling of **board_timer_update**. **MW** is optimizing the local variables in case of division and use the **accl** and **acch** registers, so if the tick interrupt happens  after performing the division operation and before using **accl** or **acch** register, there will be a corrupted values in those registers when returning from the ISR where an exception happened and the arc_reset is called.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enhancement
   Adding the following code to INTERRUPT_PROLOGUE and EXCEPTION_PROLOGUE at **arc_asm_common.h**
```
#if ARC_FEATURE_FPU_DSP_CONTEXT
	SAVE_R58_R59
	SAVE_FPU_REGS
	SAVE_DSP_REGS
#endif
```
and the following to INTERRUPT_EPILOGUE and EXCEPTION_EPILOGUE
```
#if ARC_FEATURE_FPU_DSP_CONTEXT
	RESTORE_DSP_REGS
	RESTORE_FPU_REGS
	RESTORE_R58_R59
#endif
```
same as SAVE_CALLEE_REGS and RESTORE_CALLEE_REGS.

title: applySensorNotification method raises an exception when protocol and address properties are missing in events

Some events (as TemperatureEvent, LuminosityEvent etc) don't add these properties because not related to a specific protocol. So a "IndexOutOfBoundsException" is raised.

Possible solution: add a check to retrieve these values only if the properties are present in the evaluating event.

title: Setting color values individually
body:
### Where are you using pytradfri (eg stand-alone, Home Assistant etc)
Stand-alone

### Version of pytradfri
5.2.1

### Expected behaviour
```
lamp.light_control.set_hsb(1000, 1000, None)
```
Above code should update hue and saturation, but leave brightness unchanged. This is the behaviour of 5.2.0

### Actual behaviour
Program crashes with the following (partial) traceback:

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pytradfri/device.py", line 224, in set_hsb
    self._value_validate(brightness, RANGE_BRIGHTNESS, "Brightness")
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pytradfri/device.py", line 266, in _value_validate
    if value < rnge[0] or value > rnge[1]:
TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'NoneType' and 'int'
```

In other words. The value validation I wrote blocks this type of use.

### Solution
The easiest solution is changing the value validation to allow `None` as a value. 

See PR #139

title: VSCP Works: False “Failed to update data” message"
body:
When playing around with the registers of the Beijing module, I suddenly receive a message box “Failed to update data” when I push the update button. Note that the power was NOT cycled, and the daemon was NOT restarted. This happened a couple of times within 3 hours of testing.

Heartbeats and button/ temperature events were still received.

When the VSCP Device Configurator is closed and opened again, the changed settings that I thought where not updated because of the “Failed to update data” message, are updated??

It looks like this behavior is reproducible when you go to the Decision Matrix and try to enable a row (right mouse button context menu). See attached pic.

When I use the edit box to enable a row, the update is successful without an error dialog box.

title: Light Brightness - value remebered
body:
Hi,

I have noticed a problem in Appdeamon Dashboard wherby the previous brightness level set in the dashbaord is remembered.

In more detail:
1) change a lights brightness via the dashbaord
2) adjust the same lights brightness via another means (i.e. directly in HA interface)
3) turn off the light (either via the dashboard or another means)
4) turn the light on via the dashboard.

Behaviour: the light is turned on at the brightness that was set in step 1
Expected Behavior: the light is turned on without affecting the previous brightness - ideally no brighness control sent.

I have tried to follow what is happening, however my pyton skills are not up to the level required.

title: listen_state fires every second  while state is unchanged
body:
Hi there, 
great software you have created. 

I was running into an issue where the listen_state fires every second while the state stays the same. In particular this line of code:
```
        self.listen_state(self.switch_on, entity="media_player.geluidstromer", new='playing')
```
which listen to the chromecast audio state. 
when printing the args it says:
```
('media_player.geluidstromer', 'state', 'playing', 'playing', {'handle': UUID('f...')
```
showing the state is indeed not changed.

adding the old states to the listen_state def does not trigger the callback every second:

```
        self.listen_state(self.switch_on, entity="media_player.geluidstromer", new='playing', old='idle')
```

title: No network on hassos 3.5 with dtoverlay=pi3-disable-bt
body:
I tried the update to hassos 3.5 from 2.12 on my Raspberry Pi 3B and the update ended with the network not being available anymore.

I have narrowed this down to having
```
dtoverlay=pi3-disable-bt
```
in my /boot/config.txt file to use the my Razberry Z-Wave board. This parameter was necessary to have the Z-Wave controller module available and is documented here:
https://www.home-assistant.io/docs/z-wave/device-specific/#razberry-board

Without the dtoverlay=pi3-disable-bt in config.txt my Pi is booting properly (but I suppose won't have zwave anymore.

title: MQTT $localip gets truncated
body:
Having an issue with Homie v2.0.0 MQTT $localip
it gets truncated in the last symbol when published in MQTT:
e.g. instead of "192.168.1.128" it gets "192.168.1.12", tested this on two nodes but with the same results.

This issue was addressed in the receive Gobot v1.13.0 release, the purpose of this tracking GH issue is to serve as a way for affected users to know that you need to update.

title: Error installing while using Go Modules due to outdated dependency name
body:
When installing using the instructions from https://gobot.io/ using Go 1.13 and Go Modules, an error occurs like so
```
~/workspace/dronez(master): go get -u gobot.io/x/gobot/...
...
go: github.com/codegangsta/cli@v1.22.1: parsing go.mod: unexpected module path "github.com/urfave/cli"
go: error loading module requirements
```

This is due to the usage of the dependency "github.com/codegangsta/cli", which has been renamed to "github.com/urfave/cli"

(See that https://github.com/codegangsta/cli redirects to https://github.com/urfave/cli)

https://github.com/hybridgroup/gobot/blob/635adea96f788ab19bf77470b075f8db44d34fa0/cli/main.go#L6

title: Accessory calculates AID incorrectly
body:
The Accessory module calculates AID like this:

acc.aid = len(self.accessories) + 2

However if you have previously deleted and created a new AID, the max number of that AID is higher, so it should be:

if len(self.accessories)>0:
            acc.aid = max(self.accessories) + 1
        else:
            acc.aid = 2

title: Race condition when setting timeout for send event; Document Characteristic.setter_callback
body:
Hi, 

I'm trying to set up a door (`LockMechanism` service) and I'm having this problem: the hap_server responds with timed out.

The door is initially closed and then I tap to open it within the apple's home app.

Here is what I have:

main.py
``` python
"""An example of how to setup and start an Accessory.

This is:
1. Create the Accessory object you want.
2. Add it to an AccessoryDriver, which will advertise it on the local network,
    setup a server to answer client queries, etc.
"""
import logging
import signal

from pyhap.accessories.ElectronicLock import ElectronicLock
from pyhap.accessory import Bridge
from pyhap.accessory_driver import AccessoryDriver

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

def get_accessory():
    """Call this method to get a standalone Accessory."""
    acc = ElectronicLock("Door")
    return acc

acc = get_accessory()

# Start the accessory on port 51826
driver = AccessoryDriver(acc, port=51826)
# We want KeyboardInterrupts and SIGTERM (kill) to be handled by the driver itself,
# so that it can gracefully stop the accessory, server and advertising.
signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, driver.signal_handler)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, driver.signal_handler)
# Start it!
driver.start()
```

ElectronicLock.py
```python
from pyhap.accessory import Accessory, Category
import pyhap.loader as loader

class LOCK_STATE:
   OPEN = 0
   CLOSED = 1

class ElectronicLock(Accessory):
   category = Category.DOOR

   def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
      super(ElectronicLock, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)

   def set_state(self, value):
      self.get_service("LockMechanism").get_characteristic("LockCurrentState").set_value(value)
      self.get_service("LockMechanism").get_characteristic("LockTargetState").set_value(value, should_callback=False)

   def lock_target_callback(self, value):
      if value == LOCK_STATE.OPEN:
         print("OPEN")
      else:
         print("CLOSED")
      self.set_state(value)

   def _set_services(self):
      super(ElectronicLock, self)._set_services()
      lm_service = loader.get_serv_loader().get("LockMechanism")
      self.add_service(lm_service)
      lm_service.get_characteristic("LockTargetState").setter_callback = self.lock_target_callback

```

terminal log:
```
DEBUG:pyhap.hap_server:Request PUT from address '('192.168.1.100', 54902)' for path '/characteristics'.

OPEN

INFO:pyhap.hap_server:192.168.1.100 - Request timed out: timeout('timed out',)
```

The thing is that after the time out occurs, the server seems to stop listening new requests. I took a look at `HAPServerHandler -> BaseHTTPRequestHandler` but I couldn't find anything.

Any ideas why this is happening?

title: WiFi Reconnect Issues
body:
I've had to power of/ on my thermostat a couple of times in the last 3 months as it has dropped off the network and never reconnects (MQTT messages stop working/ there is no assigned DHCP lease etc). I suspect my automated AP firmware update causes the wireless to disconnect, but the thermostat simply never reconnects and is the only IOT device (of about 30) that doesn't. No SoftAP etc, it's just offline.

The thermostat itself works, in that the relay is triggered at target temperature and the target temperature can be adjusted.

Not sure if it matters but I tend to run with webServer set to false.

Is there any way to increase logging/ send serial logs to MQTT or another source? 

Has anyone else experience issues such as this?

title: Synccontroller failed  to send deleteOperation msg to edge
body:
<!-- Please use this template while reporting a bug and provide as much info as possible. Thanks!-->
**What happened**:
Synccontroller send [deleteOperation msg](https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/blob/dc4f9398335c6ed02461b7e6b14f6169c9da4f24/cloud/pkg/synccontroller/objectsync.go#L24) with nil content，which can’t be send by cloudhub. Now Cloudhub can‘t tell [if msg is deleteMessge](https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/blob/dc4f9398335c6ed02461b7e6b14f6169c9da4f24/cloud/pkg/cloudhub/channelq/channelq.go#L92) with [nil content](https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/blob/dc4f9398335c6ed02461b7e6b14f6169c9da4f24/cloud/pkg/cloudhub/channelq/channelq.go#L356). And [edge](https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/blob/dc4f9398335c6ed02461b7e6b14f6169c9da4f24/edge/pkg/edged/edged.go#L1027) can't handle deleteOperation msg with nil content.

**What you expected to happen**:
Synccontroller send deleteOperation msg successfully.

**How to reproduce it (as minimally and precisely as possible)**:
There is objectsyncs of pod A, but pod A has been deleted in kube-apiserver.

**Anything else we need to know?**:

**Environment**:
- KubeEdge version(e.g. `cloudcore/edgecore --version`):  
build from master (commit 3c441763f433096c96d39457f2b655c38377a55f)

**CloudSide Environment**:

body:

title: Type family
body: Every device must be part of a family of device. There will be different types of lights but they will always be **lights**. You need to add a predefined (in possibilities of selections) family that identify a type and that is then passed to the device. Think if you need a tagging like system or if a device belong to a unique family. Probably the latter is the right one, and for other cases we can use groups.

title: OSP Web Server: clear cache when bundle is removed
body: Static resources in the in-memory OSP Web Server cache must be removed when the bundle providing them is removed. Currently they stay in the cache, which leads to old data being served when a bundle is upgraded.

title: Toolkit-timer: 'delay' parameter is not respected after first use and in stopped state
body:
The toolkit Timer uses *firstDelay* and *interval* parameters to control the timer behaviour. If the timer is stopped and the delay is ever re-set using **setDelay** or **setDelayAndInterval** methods, only the *interval* part is respected when the timer is restarted.

Note, that when the timer is in a running state, the methods correctly behave as expected (both are respected).

Correct behaviour would be for both parameters to be respected whether in a running or stopped state, with *firstDelay* reset every time a **setDelay___** function is used.

title: Receive corrupted ID when using multiple repeaters.
body:
Hello,

I recently installed a mysensors network in a large building on two floors. Since I have placed several repeaters, I find random problems during the associations of sensors.


## Description of the problem :

During the association, **randomly**, the sensor receives an ID longer than the 3 characters of range 0-255 normally expected.

These are often numbers 255 or 100 placed after the ID, other numbers appear or changes the ID itself, but this is more rare.

I suspect that repeaters "overwrite" the value by repeating at the same time the payload. (not sure).

The fewer repeaters in the bridge area, the less problems are manifested. (no problem without repeaters).

In the example below, the good ID sent by the gateway is 197, the sensor receives 197255 and takes ID 135 after conversion:
197255 = b110000001010000111, converted to uint8, only the last 8 bits on the right (b010000111) are retrieved, giving 135.

### For information, here is the topology of the network setup:

![img_0931](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4836635/30483613-c7ca5586-9a27-11e7-846d-1b4640149b0c.jpg)


## Proposed amendment to the code:

I have not yet figured out the exact origin of the problem, however it seems to me it would be a good idea that the protocol is resistant to this ID corruption:

In the transportAssignNodeID function of MyTransport.cpp, the test:
```
// verify if ID valid
if (newNodeId! = GATEWAY_ADDRESS && newNodeId <= AUTO) {
```
Replacing "! =" With "<=" is not enough because the conversion to uint8 returns a pseudo-random number when the ID is greater than 255;

Change to uint32 type rather than uint8 works but is not optimized and could result many side-effects.

I propose this modification based on the length of the message which has the advantage of not touching the statement of the function:

### Before:
```
bool transportAssignNodeID(const uint8_t newNodeId)
{
	// verify if ID valid
	if (newNodeId != GATEWAY_ADDRESS && newNodeId != AUTO) {
		_transportConfig.nodeId = newNodeId;
		transportSetAddress(newNodeId);
		// Write ID to EEPROM
		hwWriteConfig(EEPROM_NODE_ID_ADDRESS, newNodeId);
		TRANSPORT_DEBUG(PSTR("TSF:SID:OK,ID=%d\n"),newNodeId);	// Node ID assigned
		return true;
	} else {
		TRANSPORT_DEBUG(PSTR("!TSF:SID:FAIL,ID=%d\n"),newNodeId);	// ID is invalid, cannot assign ID
		setIndication(INDICATION_ERR_NET_FULL);
		_transportConfig.nodeId = AUTO;
		return false;
	}
}
```

### After:
```
bool transportAssignNodeID(const uint8_t newNodeId)
{
	// verify if ID corrupted
	if(mGetLength(_msg) <= 3) {
		// verify if ID valid
		if (newNodeId != GATEWAY_ADDRESS && newNodeId <= AUTO) {
			_transportConfig.nodeId = newNodeId;
			transportSetAddress(newNodeId);
			// Write ID to EEPROM
			hwWriteConfig(EEPROM_NODE_ID_ADDRESS, newNodeId);
			TRANSPORT_DEBUG(PSTR("TSF:SID:OK,ID=%d\n"),newNodeId);	// Node ID assigned
			return true;
		}
		TRANSPORT_DEBUG(PSTR("!TSF:SID:FAIL,ID=%d\n"),newNodeId);	// ID is invalid, cannot assign ID
	} else {
		TRANSPORT_DEBUG(PSTR("!TSF:SID:FAIL,CORRUPT\n"),newNodeId);	// ID is corrupted, cannot assign ID
	}
	setIndication(INDICATION_ERR_NET_FULL);
	_transportConfig.nodeId = AUTO;
	return false;
}
```

title: USART serial availableForWrite() not working
body:
There’s a simple typo in the implementation of `USARTSerial::availableForWrite()`:

``` cpp
int USARTSerial::availableForWrite(void)
{
return HAL_USART_Available_Data(_serial);
}
int USARTSerial::available(void)
{
return HAL_USART_Available_Data(_serial);
}
```

https://github.com/spark/firmware/blob/develop/wiring/src/spark_wiring_usartserial.cpp#L69

It should probably be `HAL_USART_Available_Data_For_Write`. However, if I fix that and use it with code like:

``` cpp
        Serial.printlnf("%d", Serial1.availableForWrite());
        Serial1.println("01234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789");
        Serial.printlnf("%d", Serial1.availableForWrite());
```

The output is:

```
0
50
```

I’m unclear of how the function is supposed to work. Should it:
1. Return the number of bytes waiting to be written out from the buffer (0=empty send buffer), which is what it does.
2. Return the number of bytes you can write to the buffer without blocking, which would be SERIAL_BUFFER_SIZE - the value currently returned.

Thanks,
Rick

---

title: 0.6.0/0.6.1-rc.1 System.sleep(30) never wakes
first TCP connection to softap is not closed automatically by the device.
body:
The device should close it's end of the TCP connection after sending the response data. This doesn't happen for the first connection, but does happen on subsequent connections. 

Expected outcome
```
Given I have photon in listening mode
And the computer's Wi-Fi is connected to the Photon's AP
When I run `echo -e -n "device-id\n0\n\n" | nc 192.168.0.1 5609`
Then the device should output some JSON
And the stream is closed
And When I run `echo -e -n "device-id\n0\n\n" | nc 192.168.0.1 5609` again
Then the device should output some JSON 
And the stream is closed
```

Actual outcome

```
Given I have photon in listening mode
And the computer's Wi-Fi is connected to the Photon's AP
When I run `echo -e -n "device-id\n0\n\n" | nc 192.168.0.1 5609`
Then the device should output some JSON
And the stream remains open
When I press Ctrl-C the command exits
And When I run `echo -e -n "device-id\n0\n\n" | nc 192.168.0.1 5609` again
Then the device should output some JSON
And the stream is closed
```

What happens in practice is the stream from the first command is not closed. Instead, you have to use Ctrl-C to close the stream.  Subsequent invocations of the same command then sees the device correctly close the stream automatically. 

Tested on a Photon, firmware 0.4.9 and 0.6.0

title: If WKP pin is held HIGH on deep sleep wake, RTC alarm and future LOW to HIGH transition on WKP will not wake device.
Particle.keepAlive() API does not work
Electron power-on connectivity issues with 0.8.0-rc
body:
## Bug Report

### Expected Behavior

Stay connected without an external battery.

### Observed Behavior

Upgrading to 0.8.0-rc7 has made it so our units no longer stay connected when booted off the power supply. We are using a regulated lab 5V supply rated well in excess of 5A.

This was reproduced across multiple Electrons.

Another user reported similar issues with 0.8.0-rc3.

### Steps to Reproduce

The Electrons boot and stay connected when a battery is present; but without the battery, on the first boot they do not stay connected. What’s weird is that they do complete the handshake and deliver an initial diagnostics message (we can see it in the console), but then they go silent and become unreachable. The cyan LED starts blinking rapidly.

It’s also weird that the battery can be removed and then the Electron rebooted without reproducing this problem. It really is only on initial power-on.

Using particle device doctor to downgrade to 0.7.0 solves the problem.

This occurs whether the firmware was compiled for 0.7.0 or 0.8.0-rc4 (-rc7 is not available right now as an option in Particle Dev).

This is using a 3rd party AT&T SIM, with KeepAlive set at 60 seconds.

### Test App

title: Particle.publish() flags fixes
body:
This PR sanitizes `Particle.publish()` overloads, making it possible to specify one or two flags for any variant of this method. The PR also implements a generic type-safe wrapper for enum-based flags, aka `particle::Flags<T>`, which enables regular syntax for ORed flag combinations as an option:

```cpp
Particle.publish("event", "data", PRIVATE | NO_ACK);
```

Fixes #1201 and #1194.

title: Feature/arduino compatibility regression
body:
Attempts to restore 0.6.0-style arduino compatibility as outlined by issue #1278 

In a nutshell:

- the default is 0.6.0 Arduino compatibility - let's call this partial compatibility. 
- applications and libraries include "Arduino.h" to define `ARDUINO` and bring in many more additional defines to complete the Arduino API.  We'll call this full compatibility. 


## Mixing libraries that require partial and full Arduino compatibility

Libraries that require full Arduino compatibility may include Arduino.h in their library header file, while libraries that depend on partial compatibility (because they themselves define missing symbols) don't include Arduino.h.  If a library requiring partial compatibility is included after one requiring full compatibility then the partial compatibility library may break, since Arduino symbols that it's not expecting will be defined. 

The workaround for this is to include all libraries requiring partial compatibility before libraries requiring full compatibility in the application file.

title: [USB] Fixes an issue with Serial when receiving consecutive multiple 64-byte transmissions from Host
body:
### Problem

With RX queue full, the device NAKs OUT (Host->Device) transfers. With `bInterval` set to `0` (1 ms) this causes the device to spend too much time in USB interrupt, not giving any processing time to the application/system thread to consume RX queue. For some reason CDC driver on OSX doesn't attempt to send any data after about 300 NAKed transfers. Closing/re-opening the port doesn't help either.

### Solution

Set `bInterval` to a maximum value (16 ms) on OUT endpoint to give enough time for the device to consume RX queue and not send too many NAKs to Host.

We should probably add some workaround as well: e.g. count the number of NAKs sent and silently drop incoming data after a certain threshold instead of NAKing it. Even though this is OSX driver's fault. :face_with_head_bandage:

### Steps to Test

The bug is easily reproducible on OSX when setting WPA Enterprise credentials over Serial. A simple copy-paste of a certificate (e.g. CA) will not complete and get stuck somewhere half-way through.

title: [Photon/P1] Fixes thread priority issues
body:
### Problem

- Closes #1344
- Another known issue related to this is connecting to enterprise access points with `SYSTEM_THREAD(ENABLED)`

### Solution

- Constantly holding a mutex breaks priority inheritance mechanisms in FreeRTOS. Do not block _start mutex in ActiveObjectBase
- [Photon/P1] socket_hal: Some cosmetic changes: wiced semaphores replaced by os_mutex_t, std::vector replaced with spark::Vector, SocketListLock implemented with std::lock_guard
- TCPClient: It's much better to just copy from a previously created s_invalid_client static TCPClient(socket_handle_invalid()) when there are no new connections, instead of allocating new one each call
- Updated Supplicant_BESL.a. See spark/photon-wiced#14

title: [LTE/Electron] eDRX & Power Saving mode disabled by default
body:
### Problem

eDRX is enabled by default on production u-Blox modules, and it causes a variable lag in the network data transmissions that we do not account for at this time.

### Solution

1. Force eDRX to be disabled

2. Force Power Saving mode to be disabled (for good measure)

3. This PR also adds some logs for 2G/3G devices that query the modem for the DNS servers used.  Right now there are no high level APIs implemented, but later we can add support for `Cellular.dnsServerIP()` like was done for WiFi devices: https://docs.particle.io/reference/firmware/photon/#dnsserverip-

4. This PR also increases the OTA update timeout on Electron/LTE devices to 300 seconds (up from 90).  This allows LTE devices on the lossier Cat-M1 network to have a better chance of receiving resent `UpdateDone` messages.

### Steps to Test

Manual testing required:
1. Flash LTE device with this system firmware and tinker
2. Make a function call `particle call <device> digitalwrite D7,LOW`
3. It should return 1 if successful
4. Wait 3 minutes
5. Make a function call `particle call <device> digitalwrite D7,LOW`
6. It should return 1 if successful

body:
### Problem
Pin A5 stays high when using Ethernet feather wing on Xenon. 

**ISSUE LINK:**
https://community.particle.io/t/pin-a5-stays-high-when-using-ethernet-feather-wing-on-xenon/47646

Ethernet driver wants to control the SPI SS pin itself, it passes 0xffff to SPI driver, but 0xffff means SPI_DEFAULT_SS, here we use PIN_INVALID(0xff).

    pinMode(A5, OUTPUT);
}

    delay(100);

}

title: [Gen 2] Fixes thread-safety issues in libc functions
[Photon/P1] Fixes handling of invalid WiFi access point config entries in DCT
body:
### Problem

1. See #1968, WiFi AP list gets initialized to 0xff instead of expected 0x00, which causes invalid (filled with 0xff) entries to be treated as valid credentials resulting in crashes
2. There aren't any sanity checks on the WiFi AP entries retrieved from DCT

### Solution

1. Fill the stored AP list with 0x00 in factory DCT image file: `dct_prep.bin`. Previously this list should have been initialized with `NVMEM_SPARK_Reset_SysFlag` system flag set, however (unconfirmed, most likely) due to restructuring of the factory image in #1887 and `NVMEM_SPARK_Reset_SysFlag` being used to perform a factory reset in the bootloader (after which it's reset), the system no longer runs `wlan_reset_credentials_store()`.
2. Add sanity checks to `wlan_hal`.

### Steps to Test

#### Sanity checks
1. Flash https://github.com/particle-iot/device-os/files/3877387/dct_dump_full.bin.zip @ 0x08004000 using the debugger
2. Flash stock DeviceOS
3. The device should crash after failing to connect to the WiFi AP
4. Flash DeviceOS out of this branch
5. The device should not crash

#### Factory image

Follow the steps in #1887, get the device to boot into DeviceOS, put into DFU mode, get a full two-page DCT dump (`dfu-util -d 2b04:c006 -s 0x08004000:0x8000 -a 0 -U dct_dump_full.bin`), make sure that `0x4a30` - `0x4a90` and `0x8a30` - `0x8a90` are zero-initialized.

N/A

title: Fix CGI for Quectel modems
body:
submission notes ``` **Important:** Please sanitize/remove any confidential info like usernames, passwords, org names, product names/ids, access tokens, client ids/secrets, or anything else you don't wish to share. Please Read and Sign the Contributor License Agreement ([Info here](https://github.com/spark/firmware/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md)). You may also delete this submission notes header if you'd like. Thank you for contributing! ```
### Problem Quectel modems do not correctly report CGI data. `quectel_ncp_client.cpp` appears to have been copied before CGI data was finished. This results in not setting the flags TWO_DIGIT_MNC flag value, and the lac and ci are reported from uninitialized values. ### Solution Copy the latest Gen3 implementation from `sara_ncp_client.cpp`. ### Steps to Test Create a sample application on new b5som hardware ### Example App ```c void setup() { Particle.publishVitals(15); } void loop() { } ``` ### References [ch46950](https://app.clubhouse.io/particle/story/46950/quectel-modems-do-not-correctly-report-cgi-data) --- ### Completeness - [x] User is totes amazing for contributing! - [x] Contributor has signed CLA ([Info here](https://github.com/spark/firmware/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md)) - [x] Problem and Solution clearly stated - [x] Run unit/integration/application tests on device - [ ] Added documentation - [ ] Added to CHANGELOG.md after merging (add links to docs and issues)

title: Fix the monitor orientation code for rotated cameras

ZM has changed its code on how to handle rotation - this results in squished images

Can be fixed with a check to scale to height or to width according program window size.

title: Cloud Sync issue
body:
It appears that our much-hated and struggled cloud sync algorithm is flawed, and leaves gaps in the data
This is what graphs on the source computer look like
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/12278048/18058398/19a51b64-6e0c-11e6-9a0b-bc73535fb990.png)
And this is what graphs on the destination computer look like

In addition, the app is currently in a consistent refuse-to-sync state, meaning that for 5 days now no new data made it to the server. However the serve is displaying successful REST requests with no errors. 
Furthermore, streaming of real-time data has failed as well, somehow. We appear to be in a 'shit's fucked' situation.

title: Tomcat module fails during installation
body:
The tomcat module fails during the middleware installation. However, when the installation is limited to tomcat using

    python smartcity-middleware.py install -f middleware.conf -l tomcat
It succeeds. It has now become necessary to do this as the tomcat installation fails every single time the middleware installation is carried out.

title: Pushpin: build process takes very long time
body:
### Submission type
- [x] Bug report
- [ ] Request for enhancement (RFE)

### Middleware version the issue has been seen with
> smartcity-middleware v0.1.0.alpha

### Bug Report
Pushpin takes a very long time to build. This could be because of `make` or `git` commands used in the Dockerfile.

title: Fix: missing war file in config/tomcat directory
body: The new war file was placed in `config` directory instead of `config/tomcat` directory.

title: Shutdown of socket inbound datasource shows error in logs
body: When the server shuts down, the socket event source generates an exception on closing the socket even though the condition is expected. The error should be ignored if the event source is shutting down.

title: Support "administer own tenant" permission in REST services and web interface
body: Though there is already a permission for "administer own tenant" in the list available for users, the REST services and web interface do not use the permission. The permission should be checked when querying for a single tenant by id, updating a tenant, listing existing tenants, and deleting a tenant via the REST services. It should also apply to the administration dropdown to determine whether the "Manage Tenants" entry is made available.

title: [RFC] Support conditional off commands base on operation/fan/temperature
body:
See https://github.com/smartHomeHub/SmartIR/issues/63

The IR codes update is just an example,  offFlags should be set `["operation", "fan"]` for Gree. I'll update the IR codes once the python code is settled.

Feel free to change wording, English is not my primary language.

title: problem parsing relative items in conf when combined with a function
body:
When using relative items in combination with a function, lets say a "changed_by()" or "last_update()" the relative item gets misinterpreted.

Easiest way to solve I guess is to search for () and then interpret the dots differently.
sh...changed_by() should look for the change of the item itself.. though having three consecutive points looks a bit weird ;)

title: Change the Identification of the Devices.
body: Early in the development of the IoT Agent, the library API was thought to use the combination of deviceId and deviceType as a key to distinguish different devices. This assumption doesn't hold anymore, so it's time to change it.

title: Fix transport for an autoprovisioned device
body:
Since transport is not set, default transport is always MQTT by conf.

i.e.:
```
curl -i -X GET 'http://localhost:7896/iot/d?k=rfz7xmuusvf9ikdv23wf7fmhv&i=disp25&d=|temperature|4&'
```
This is creating a device without transport defined, but we know that transport is HTTP

title: [BUG]
body:
**Description**
I'm taking a look to the this gateway and thingboard server.
I'm getting errors when polling modbus (TCP). 
1- It uses wrong function code when polling. It sends 4 (Read Input Register) when it must send 3 (Read Holding Register). I've checked that using wireshark
2 - It logs some error about response.
3 - It doesn't publish in MQTT (I guess because 1 and 2)

**Connector name:**
Modbus Connector

**Error traceback (If available):**
conector log show this records (repeated)
''2020-02-16 18:52:33' - INFO - modbus_connector - 59 - Modbus connected.'
''2020-02-16 18:52:34' - DEBUG - modbus_connector - 198 - To modbus device Medidor Potencia, 
{'byteOrder': 'LITTLE', 'tag': 'valCount', 'type': 'long', 'functionCode': 3, 'registerCount': 2, 'address': 2, 'deviceName': 'Medidor Potencia'}'
''2020-02-16 18:52:34' - DEBUG - modbus_connector - 199 - With result ReadRegisterResponse (2)'

title: Sunroof control (not working on Tesla's end)
body:
After lots of playing around, I've been able to use almost all of the documented API calls EXCEPT the sun_roof_control. 

Whether I'm using the interface provided by Tim Dorr or a simple (ish) Java application I've written, I can't seem to find any combination of sunroof calls parameters that work. States of "close", "open", "comfort", "move" all return 400 errors (`{"response": null,  "error": "invalid_state", "error_description": ""}` but "vent" returns a 200 but with the following: `{"response": {"reason": "unknown state requested: ", "result": false}}`. Results seem to be the same with and without "percent" provided. 

I'm definitely working on a car that has a panosunroof. I'm not sure it should make any difference, but the car is about 2 weeks out from delivery so it has the newest hardware. When I do a  data_request/vehicle_state on the vehicle, I get the following back (among other parts): `"sun_roof_installed": 2,"sun_roof_percent_open": 0,"sun_roof_state": "unknown",`

Does anyone know of any differences in the API for the sunroof? Are there other combinations of parameters that anyone has had any success with.

Thanks so much for any help you can give!

body:
### Expected behavior
The streaming API works as intended, and returns updates every ~250 msec.

### Actual behavior
The streaming API connects successfully, but does not return anything regardless of the state of the Model 3 (awake, driving, etc - nothing is returned).

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
This started as soon as my Model 3 updated to version 2018.18.3 04dfd3c. Streaming was working before that, then immediately after the update, it began returning nothing. I've restarted the car, restarted the server, tried across several different days, and the streaming API continues to return nothing.

title: unify responses for 'data not found' from plugin
body:
## Description
It seems like when a device does not have any data (e.g. the plugin SDK fails to read anything off of a configured device) we either:
- get `"null"` back as the reading value in a valid 200 read response
- get a 500 specifying that no readings are found

The behavior here should be unified so we always expect the same thing for the same class of error. I'm not sure what conditions make it so that "null" is returned vs. a 500, so that will need to be investigated first.

To recreate this, there can be a device configured in a plugin that doesn't physically exist, so the SDK can't read off of it. Then read that device off of Synse Server.

I think that returning "null" (or the actual `null` type) should be fine, but I'd like to hear what others think -- does it seem like returning `null` is better, or returning a 500 error when there are no readings for a configured device.

Note that this case of 'no readings' can occur when:
- a device is configured but doesnt exist
- a device is unplugged/unreachable
- a device fails and can no longer be read from

title: runtime: the url object should be in URI-encoded in the `openUrl()`
body:
<!--
Thank you for reporting a possible bug in YodaRT.

Please fill in as much of the template below as you can.

Version: output of `getprop | grep ro.rokid.build.yodaos | awk '{ print $2 }'`
Platform: output of `uname -a`
Subsystem: if known, please specify the affected core module name

If possible, please provide code that demonstrates the problem, keeping it as
simple and free of external dependencies as you can.
-->

* **Version**: next
* **Platform**: all
* **Subsystem**: runtime

<!-- Please provide more details below this comment. -->
Here is the example to reproduce this problem:

```js
rt.openUrl('yoda-app://telephony?from=+3678')
```
Then the `url.query` object should be:

```js
{ from: ' 3678' }
```

A possible solution should call `encodeURI` or `encodeURIComponent` before calling the parse function.
